findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The title slide layout is significantly simplified in HTML. The PDF has a full-page cover layout with panoramic images at top and bottom, the NC logo, title text, author info, and date arranged in a specific visual composition. The HTML strips most of this layout structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a visually rich cover page with two panoramic photos framing the content. HTML shows the images and logo stacked vertically with minimal layout, losing the integrated cover page design.

findings.description: The title and author text from the cover slide is completely missing from the HTML rendering of page 1.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows '15A NCAC 02K .0224 - Additional Requirements for Dams that Impound Coal Combustion Residuals' and author info 'Andrew Brooks, PE' etc. The HTML page 1 rendering shows only the images and NC logo with no title or author text visible.

findings.description: The presentation slide layout with image on the right and bullet points on the left (page 2/slide 2) is not preserved. In the HTML, the image appears above the bullet list rather than beside it.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a two-column-style layout with bullet points (Purpose, Methodology, Major Components) on the left and a photo on the right. HTML stacks the image above the bullet points.

findings.description: On the HTML rendering of page 2, content from the next slide (Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Purpose) begins appearing below the DEMLR Dam Safety Rulemaking content, merging two slides together.
findings.evidence: PDF has 'DEMLR Dam Safety Rulemaking' as a standalone slide. HTML shows the heading 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Purpose' starting at the bottom of the same section, merging slides 2 and 3.

findings.description: Content from multiple slides is merged together in the HTML rendering of pages 3-4, with slide boundaries not clearly delineated.
findings.evidence: PDF shows separate slides for 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Methodology' (slides 4 and 5). HTML merges the content of these slides together with content from surrounding slides into a continuous flow.

findings.description: The Regulatory Impact Analysis is presented as a simple aligned list in the PDF but converted to a table in the HTML. While the data values are preserved, the table adds column structure (headers implied by HTML comment but no actual <th> row) that does not exist in the source PDF. This is a minor structural change but acceptable. However, the table lacks header rows which is a minor formatting issue.
findings.evidence: PDF shows aligned text pairs (e.g., 'State Government:' aligned with 'Minimal Costs'). HTML wraps these in a <table> with an HTML comment '<!-- COLUMNS: Entity/Impact | Status -->' but no <th> elements.

findings.description: The second occurrence of 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Methodology' in the PDF (page 5) is rendered as a duplicate h2 heading in the HTML. While this faithfully mirrors the PDF's slide structure, it results in two identical h2 headings which could be confusing in a non-presentation context.
findings.evidence: Both PDF page 4 and page 5 have the title 'Dam Safety CCR Rulemaking Methodology'. HTML has two <h2> elements with identical text.

findings.description: There are two nested <main> elements in the HTML, which is a structural duplication artifact.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<main>' inside '<body>' then another '<main>' nested inside, creating duplicate main landmarks.
